content:
Incident Report
Page 2 of 11
DOB: 03/21/81 Address: 1717 Pacific Ave Tacoma, WA 98402
Race/Sex: /M Work Phone:
Home Phone: (253)365-5705 Employer:
NARRATIVE: Name: Engelbert K AGENCY CASE NUMBERS :
Lewis County Sheriff's Office: 20C11252 Grays Harbor County Sheriff's Office: 20-16695 Thurston County Sheriff's Office: 20-4433 Washington State Patrol: 20-014825
NARRATIVE: On 09-03-2020 at approximately 2000 hours, I, Detective Sergeant Kevin Engelbertson, was contacted by Lieutenant Ray Brady of the Thurston County Sheriff's Office. Lieutenant Brady advised me that the Region 3 Critical Incident Investigation Team (CIIT) was being activated for a shooting at 7601 3rd Way Southeast in Lacey, Washington.
I was informed there were possibly up to four shooting officers involved and multiple witness officers who had been on scene during the incident. I was also informed the suspect, Michael Reinoehl, was deceased, had not been transported and was still at the scene. I was further informed that detectives from Grays Harbor County, the Washington State Patrol and Lewis County would be conducting the investigation with Thurston County being the lead agency. I was advised Mason County most likely would not be responding due to an officer involved shooting several days prior where they were the lead agency and were still investigating the case.
I called Detective Tyson Brown and Detective Scott Robb and advised them of the situation and asked that they respond to the scene.
While en route, I was contacted by Detective Sergeant Simper with the Thurston County Sheriff's Office who asked if we could divert to the Lacey Police Department where all of the shooting and witness officers were located. Our task was to process the officers for evidence and obtain statements from the witness officers, if they agreed to do so.
When I arrived at the Lacey Police Department I met the lead investigator for Thurston County, Detective Mickey Hamilton. I was present with Detective Hamilton when we addressed all of the officers and advised them what our role would be in the investigation and what to expect. We were also advised that many of the officers involved also had peer support members as well as legal representation present.
During this time I was advised there were several fulltime United States Marshals involved in the incident and possibly some officers who were cross-commissioned as TFO's with the U. S. Marshals Service. I only recognized one U.S. Marshal member, Ryan Kimmel, as I had worked with him over a year ago
rplwdir.x1
09/14/20
